notes: Backside (BS) names the symposium-modifier applied to the downtime component of a base trick (e.g., BS Atomsmasher = Atomic Symp Mirage). Pt9 Red ruling: backside = symposium-equivalent modifier, +1 ADD.
Frontside (FS) names the symposium-modifier applied to the uptime component (FS Atomsmasher = Symp Atomic Mirage). Mirror of backside.
Full Symp = both uptime + downtime symposium (Full Symp Atomsmasher = Symp Atomic Symp Mirage).
Symposium = jump-and-land on the active leg; symple = starts symposium but the non-symple foot returns to ground midway. Shorthand 'symp' covers both.
Muted = active leg held in the air for the entirety of a component without putting it on the ground. Distinct from symposium (which can plant after) and symple (which plants midway).
Alpha/Beta/Gamma refer to individual symposium dexes within a trick (alpha = first dex symposium, beta = second). Alternate to Frontside/Backside; less commonly used.
Both involve hip pivoting between dexes; paradox is for clipper-set tricks (XBD-prefix), xdex is for toe-set tricks. Paradox = +1 universal; xdex = +1 narrow (pt1: specific named tricks only).
Both are head-direction body modifiers; ducking = head moves toward bag, bag falls opposite; diving = head moves over bag, bag falls same-side. Distinct ADD contribution (+1 each).
Both move head toward bag; ducking sends bag to opposite side, weaving sends bag to same side.
Ducking + Zulu both involve over-and-under head motion; zulu sends bag to opposite side (vs diving's same-side); both are full-arc variants of the ducking family.
Cluster of four head-motion body modifiers: ducking (toward bag, falls opposite), diving (over bag, falls same-side), weaving (toward bag, falls same-side), zulu (over bag, falls opposite).
Clipper IS an inside-stall in cross-body (xbd) position. The terms clipper-stall and xbd-inside-stall are equivalent.
The two most foundational delay surfaces; nearly every trick starts or ends on toe or clipper. Toe = top-of-foot stall; Clipper = inside-stall in xbd position.
Both are body-rotation modifiers measured in degrees; gyro = 180°, spinning = 360°. Combined names produce arbitrary degrees: spinning gyro = 540°, double spinning = 720°.
Both are 360° rotations; spinning = backspin (back passes bag first), inspin = chest passes bag first. Direction prefix in- changes any rotational modifier.
Double spinning = 720° (two full rotations). Triple spinning = 1080°. Productive-multiplicity exception per pt8.
Miraging is the +1 body modifier whose application produces compounds. Drifter = miraging-clipper; torque = miraging-osis; DLO = miraging-legover. Distinct from mirage (the 2-ADD trick).
Barraging is the +1 universal body modifier; barrage is a standalone 3-ADD trick in its own family. Naming proximity is a common source of confusion.
Terraging is the +3 body modifier (pt6); terrage is a standalone 4-ADD trick. Parallel naming pattern to barraging:barrage.
Both are set-primitive modifiers with the same ADD bonus structure: +1 non-rotational, +2 rotational. Functionally parallel; named separately.
Nuclear = paradox + atomic per pt10 Red ruling. +2 ADD universal. Structurally combines atomic's set discipline with paradox's hip-pivot pattern.
Both are +1-universal set modifiers. Distinct named modifiers with same ADD profile; mechanical distinction (where the set energy comes from) varies by source.
Quantum = +1 universal; shooting = +3 (pt7 ruling). Both are set modifiers but live at opposite ADD-contribution scales.
Both are zero-ADD set modifiers per Red rulings. Pogo per pt6; rooted per pt8. The first set modifiers to NOT contribute ADD.
Both are +1 body modifiers involving the support foot; pt3 Red ruling explicitly distinguishes them. Stepping = step action interleaved; tapping = foot-pattern variant.
Blur's canonical decomposition per pt10 Red ruling: blur = stepping + paradox + mirage. Three-modifier expansion of a single-token trick name.
Torque's canonical decomposition per pt11 Red ruling: torque = miraging-osis. Cross-references the miraging body-modifier family.
Blender's canonical decomposition per pt11 Red ruling: blender = whirling-osis. Mirrors torque's pattern.
Drifter's canonical decomposition per pt11 Red ruling: drifter = miraging-clipper.
Ripwalk's canonical decomposition per pt11 Red ruling: ripwalk = stepping-butterfly. Despite Jobs-notation form 'BLURRY BUTTERFLY' in the DB notation column.
Barfry's canonical decomposition: nuclear-butterfly (with ss execution direction; ss=+0 per pt12 Red ruling).
Eggbeater's canonical decomposition per pt4 Red ruling: eggbeater = atomic-legover.
Merkon's family flipped to legover base per pt5 Red ruling. (Not in active dictionary; flagged for memory.)
Fury's canonical decomposition per pt6 Red ruling: fury = furious + paradox + mirage = 2 + 1 + 2 = 5 ADD.
Mobius's canonical reading: spinning-torque. Transitively: spinning + miraging + osis (via torque = miraging-osis). Multi-layer equivalence example.
Phoenix's technical name per PassBack glossary: Pixie Ducking Butterfly. Names the modifiers in canonical decomposition order.
ADD = Additional Degree of Difficulty. Scoring + categorization system from 1980s; controversial but ingrained.
ADD categories per PassBack: DEL (delay), DEX (dexterity), XBD (cross-body), BOD (body — spinning/ducking/paradox/symp/flying/xdex), UNS (unusual surfaces).
ADD-tier ladder: Tilts (1), Tiltless (≥2), Guilts (2), Guiltless (≥3), Tripless (≥4), Fearless (≥5), Beastly (≥6), Godly (≥7).
BOP = Butterfly + Osis + Pdx Mirage; the three foundational guiltless tricks. Genuine guiltless = guiltless excluding BOPs.
Jobs notation = written form of a trick's components in phase + side. The 'technical name' uses simplest named components; Jobs notation is the formal written form.
Alpine X = take base trick, divide uptime/downtime, insert a duck or dive between. Whether duck or dive depends on whether downtime is op (→ duck) or ss (→ dive).
Crispy X = take part of downtime phase, re-time into uptime. Ex: crispy DLO = blurry-legover (mirage moved from downtime to uptime). Rarely used in practice.
PS X = Paradox Symposium X (ex: PS Whirl = Paradox Symposium Whirl).
Same-side (ss) = near; opposite (op) = far. Interchangeable. Pt12 Red ruling: ss = +0 ADD universally.
Opposite (op) = far. Interchangeable per glossary convention.
Two dex execution styles: hippy = dex motion from hip (thigh circles bag), leggy = dex from knee (calf circles bag).
Two dex directions relative to body center: in-out (moves away from body), out-in (moves toward body). Mirage = in-out; illusion = out-in.
Full dex = leg circles bag entirely; half dex = leg circles bag partially.
Shred = playing freestyle with bag; air shred = practicing trick motions without bag.
Two senses of 'circle': (1) playing with others in a literal circle; (2) competitive event format mimicking circle play.
String = run = continuous flow of tricks in stream-of-consciousness style. Combo = shorter, themed grouping. Link = specifically a 2-trick combo.
LIMP = Legover, Illusion, Mirage, Pickup. CLIMP = LIMP + Clipper. The basic toe-trick set.
Player stance terms: bigfoot = strong toe and clipper use same foot; sideways = strong toe and clipper use same side (different feet).
Dex window terms: clean = within window; deep = high in window (far from shoe); thin = close to shoe edge; very thin = 'shoey'.
Handcatch = trick ended with bag caught in hand. Seal = hit with enough control to continue. Both imply successful execution.
Drop = bag hits ground (failure). Bail = trick modified on the fly to easier version. Footed = bag rolled off stalling foot. Over-dexed = leg dexes too far, misses bag.
BSOS = both sides one string; BSOR = both sides one run. Interchangeable; sometimes jokingly written 'bee sauce'.
Combo structures: drill = unique combo repeated other-side; rewind = combo reversed at midpoint; mirror = link where trick reverses-equivalent.
The 'the' component (pronounced 'thuh') = component attempted but completely missed. Specifically named.
Pulled set = bag dragged through uptime dex/spin before intended component. Also called slurry. Froggy = specifically for pulled spins.
Shuffle combo = tricks linked using uptime dexing sets.
Trials = combining traditional tricks with physical obstacles.
Non-freestyle disciplines: footbag net (volleyball-like over net), footbag golf (disc-golf-like), 2/4/N-square (schoolyard variants).
Passback = giving previous player another turn. Self-serve = serving to oneself; debated etiquette.
Toe scrape / toe up = sliding bag onto foot using other shoe's sole. Considered potentially disrespectful + can damage bag.
Battle = 1-on-1 technical showdown. Shred-Off = online-only async variant.
Sick 1 = best single trick. Sick 3 = best 3-trick combo. Both are best-trick competition formats.
Three endurance variations: Rippin' Run (head-to-head; named after Rick Reese), Iron Man (head-to-head), Last Man Standing (all-vs-all).
Event types: tournament (competition-focused) vs jam (casual; regional get-together).
